component.name: MetricStrip
component.description: A flat, bordered container displaying related metrics as columns separated by vertical dividers. Less emphasized than StatCard — no elevation, no individual card chrome.

useCases: Billing summaries showing charges, revenue, and remaining period as a single cohesive concept
Header subsections with always-visible contextual metrics
Order summaries with related data points (subtotal, tax, total)
Account status displays where metrics comprise a singular concept

commonPatterns.name: with-header
commonPatterns.description: Metric strip with a title row and description
commonPatterns.composition: <MetricStrip><MetricStripHeader title="This Period" description="Jan 01 – Jan 31, 2026" /><MetricStripContent><MetricStripItem value="$4,847" label="Estimated charges" /><MetricStripItem value="$484,723" label="Revenue processed" /><MetricStripItem value="19 days" label="Remaining" /></MetricStripContent></MetricStrip>

commonPatterns.name: without-header
commonPatterns.description: Metric strip with just the data row, no title
commonPatterns.composition: <MetricStrip><MetricStripContent><MetricStripItem value="$120" label="Subtotal" /><MetricStripItem value="$9.60" label="Tax" /><MetricStripItem value="$129.60" label="Total" /></MetricStripContent></MetricStrip>

antiPatterns.scenario: Using MetricStrip for independent metrics that don't relate to each other
antiPatterns.reason: MetricStrip implies the values are facets of a single concept. Unrelated KPIs should be individual StatCards in a StatsGrid.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use StatCard + StatsGrid for independent dashboard KPIs

antiPatterns.scenario: Using MetricStrip when metrics need trend indicators, actions, or expandable detail
antiPatterns.reason: MetricStrip is intentionally simple — no trends, no actions, no expansion. It is a read-only summary.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use StatCard (for trends/actions) or MetricPanel (for expandable detail)

antiPatterns.scenario: Using StatCard/StatsGrid when all the metrics relate to one concept and should be de-emphasized
antiPatterns.reason: Individual cards with elevation add visual weight. If the data is contextual background info, MetricStrip keeps it subtle.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use MetricStrip for cohesive, always-visible, low-emphasis metric displays

slots.MetricStripHeader: Optional title row with heading and description, followed by a horizontal divider
slots.MetricStripContent: Horizontal row of MetricStripItem children separated by vertical dividers
slots.MetricStripItem: A single metric column with a data value and a label

responsive.mobile: Consider stacking or reducing to fewer columns on narrow viewports
responsive.desktop: Horizontal row of equal-width metric columns

accessibility.role: Generic container — no special ARIA roles needed
accessibility.screenReader: Values and labels are read in source order

rules: Use MetricStrip when the metrics comprise a singular concept (e.g. billing period summary)
Use StatCard + StatsGrid when metrics are independent KPIs that each stand alone
Use MetricPanel when metrics need expandable detail content (charts, tables)
MetricStrip has no elevation — it is intentionally less emphasized than StatCard
MetricStripHeader is optional — omit for simple data-only displays
All MetricStripItem children go inside a single MetricStripContent — the content component handles vertical dividers automatically

aiHints.context: Use for low-emphasis, always-visible metric summaries where values relate to each other as one concept. Unlike StatCard (elevated, independent) or MetricPanel (expandable), MetricStrip is flat and compact — just a bordered container with vertical dividers. Common in header subsections and billing summaries. Uses data-sm for values, p-sm for labels, border for dividers.
